159	// ─── File-local helper: is this a Lua (non-C) closure? ───────────────────────
160	
161	// C: #define LuaClosure(f) ((f) != NULL && (f)->c.tt == LUA_VLCL)
162	// macros.tsv: LUA_VLCL → LuaClosure::Lua(_)
163	#[inline]
164	fn is_lua_closure(cl: Option<&LuaClosure>) -> bool {
165	    // C: (f) != NULL && (f)->c.tt == LUA_VLCL
166	    matches!(cl, Some(LuaClosure::Lua(_)))
167	}
168	
169	// ─── Current-PC helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
170	
171	/// Returns the program counter (0-based instruction index) for the current
172	/// instruction in call frame `ci`.
173	///
174	/// C: `static int currentpc(CallInfo *ci)`
175	/// ```c
176	/// lua_assert(isLua(ci));
177	/// return pcRel(ci->u.l.savedpc, ci_func(ci)->p);
178	/// ```
179	///
180	/// PORT NOTE: In C, `savedpc` is a pointer to the *next* instruction. `pcRel`
181	/// subtracts the code base and then subtracts 1 more to get the *current*
182	/// instruction. In Rust, `saved_pc()` stores the 0-based index of the next
183	/// instruction, so the current instruction index is `saved_pc() - 1`.
184	fn current_pc(ci: &CallInfo) -> i32 {
185	    // C: lua_assert(isLua(ci))
186	    debug_assert!(ci.is_lua());
187	    // C: pcRel(ci->u.l.savedpc, ci_func(ci)->p)
188	    // macros.tsv: pcRel → (pc - proto.code_base()) as i32 - 1
189	    // In Rust savedpc is a u32 offset into code[]; current = savedpc - 1
190	    ci.saved_pc().saturating_sub(1) as i32
191	}
192	
193	// ─── Line-info lookup ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
194	
195	/// Finds the "base line" entry in `f.abslineinfo` for instruction `pc`.
196	///
197	/// Sets `*basepc` to the pc of the base entry (or -1 if starting from the
198	/// function's first line), and returns the line number at that base.
199	///
200	/// C: `static int getbaseline(const Proto *f, int pc, int *basepc)`
201	fn get_baseline(f: &LuaProto, pc: i32, basepc: &mut i32) -> i32 {
202	    // C: if (f->sizeabslineinfo == 0 || pc < f->abslineinfo[0].pc)
203	    if f.abslineinfo.is_empty() || pc < f.abslineinfo[0].pc {
204	        // C: *basepc = -1; return f->linedefined;
205	        *basepc = -1;
206	        return f.linedefined;
207	    }
208	    // C: int i = cast_uint(pc) / MAXIWTHABS - 1;
209	    // macros.tsv: cast_uint(x) → x as u32
210	    let mut i = (pc as u32 / MAX_IWTH_ABS as u32).saturating_sub(1) as usize;
211	    // C: lua_assert(i < 0 || (i < f->sizeabslineinfo && f->abslineinfo[i].pc <= pc))
212	    debug_assert!(
213	        i < f.abslineinfo.len() && f.abslineinfo[i].pc <= pc,
214	        "getbaseline: estimate is not a lower bound"
215	    );
216	    // C: while (i + 1 < f->sizeabslineinfo && pc >= f->abslineinfo[i + 1].pc) i++;
217	    while i + 1 < f.abslineinfo.len() && pc >= f.abslineinfo[i + 1].pc {
218	        i += 1;
219	    }
220	    // C: *basepc = f->abslineinfo[i].pc; return f->abslineinfo[i].line;
221	    *basepc = f.abslineinfo[i].pc;
222	    f.abslineinfo[i].line
223	}
224	
225	/// Returns the source line number corresponding to instruction `pc` in proto `f`.
226	/// Returns -1 if the proto has no debug line information.
227	///
228	/// C: `int luaG_getfuncline(const Proto *f, int pc)` (LUAI_FUNC)
229	pub(crate) fn get_func_line(f: &LuaProto, pc: i32) -> i32 {
230	    // C: if (f->lineinfo == NULL) return -1;
231	    if f.lineinfo.is_empty() {
232	        return -1;
233	    }
234	    let mut basepc: i32 = 0;
235	    let mut baseline = get_baseline(f, pc, &mut basepc);
236	    // C: while (basepc++ < pc) { assert != ABSLINEINFO; baseline += f->lineinfo[basepc]; }
237	    // PORT NOTE: C uses post-increment `basepc++` in the condition; the body
238	    // then uses the already-incremented value. Rewritten as pre-increment.
239	    while basepc < pc {
240	        basepc += 1;
241	        // C: lua_assert(f->lineinfo[basepc] != ABSLINEINFO)
242	        debug_assert!(
243	            f.lineinfo[basepc as usize] != ABS_LINE_INFO,
244	            "get_func_line: hit ABSLINEINFO in incremental walk"
245	        );
246	        // C: baseline += f->lineinfo[basepc]
247	        baseline += f.lineinfo[basepc as usize] as i32;
248	    }
249	    baseline
250	}
251	
252	/// Returns the source line for the current instruction in call frame `ci`.
253	///
254	/// C: `static int getcurrentline(CallInfo *ci)`
255	fn get_current_line(ci: &CallInfo, state: &LuaState) -> i32 {
256	    // C: return luaG_getfuncline(ci_func(ci)->p, currentpc(ci));
257	    let proto = ci_lua_proto(ci, state);
258	    get_func_line(&proto, current_pc(ci))
259	}
260	
261	// ─── Hook support ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
262	
263	/// Sets the `trap` flag on every active Lua call frame so that the VM checks
264	/// debug hooks before each instruction.
265	///
266	/// C: `static void settraps(CallInfo *ci)`
267	///
268	/// PORT NOTE: In C this walks an intrusive doubly-linked list. In Rust,
269	/// `LuaState.call_stack` is a `Vec<CallInfo>`, so we iterate the slice.

1	//! Auxiliary library: helper functions for building Lua libraries.
2	//!
3	//! C source: `reference/lua-5.4.7/src/lauxlib.c` (1127 lines, ~50 functions)
4	//! Target crate: `lua-stdlib`
5	//!
6	//! This module provides the high-level `luaL_*` API layer that sits on top of
7	//! the raw `lua_*` C API. In Rust we translate each `luaL_*` function as a
8	//! free function receiving `&mut LuaState` rather than a method, matching the
9	//! structure of the other stdlib modules.
10	//!
11	//! PORT NOTE: The C buffer system (`luaL_Buffer`) uses a small inline initial
12	//! buffer backed by a Lua-stack userdata box on overflow. In Rust we replace
13	//! this with a plain `Vec<u8>` (`LuaBuffer`), dropping all the C-internal
14	//! `UBox` / `resizebox` / `boxgc` / `boxmt` / `newbox` / `buffonstack`
15	//! machinery. The public interface remains compatible.
16	//!
17	//! PORT NOTE: File-loading functions (`load_filex`) reference `std::fs` which
18	//! is banned outside `lua-cli`. Those functions carry `TODO(port)` markers.
19	
20	// TODO(port): LuaState, LuaValue, LuaError, GcRef, LuaString, LuaUserData,
21	// LuaDebug, and LuaType are defined across lua-vm / lua-types. Imports will be
22	// wired in Phase B. Using local stubs for Phase A so rustc can parse the file.
23	
24	use lua_types::{
25	    error::LuaError,
26	    value::LuaValue,
27	    gc::GcRef,
28	    string::LuaString,
29	    userdata::LuaUserData,
30	    LuaType,
31	    LuaStatus,
32	    arith::ArithOp,
33	};
34	use crate::state_stub::{LuaState, LuaStateStubExt as _, lua_CFunction, upvalue_index, CompareOp, LuaDebug};
35	
36	// ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37

39	/// C: `#define LEVELS1 10`
40	const LEVELS1: i32 = 10;

**Report**

- **`push_where` location

:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:100` (trait default).
- **What it does:** Pushes the source
-location prefix string (`short_src:currentline: `) for st
ack frame `level` onto the stack; empty string if no info. Mirrors `luaL_where`

in `lauxlib.c:217`.

**Family members ALSO implemented** (all as `impl LuaState

StubExt for LuaState` overrides in the same file):

- `push_f
ail` — pushes `LuaValue::Nil` (Lua 5.4 "fail" is nil); m

irrors `luaL_pushfail`.
- `push_registry` — pushes `state.registry_value()`; mirrors `lua_pushvalue(L

, LUA_REGISTRYINDEX)`.
- `push_upvalue(i)` — `lua_v

m::api::push_value(self, upvalue_index(i))`; mirrors `lua_pushvalue(L, lua_upvalueindex(i))`.
- `pop_bytes
` — pops and returns the popped value's bytes if it's a `LuaValue::Str`, else `Vec::new()`. Used by `where_error`

/ `auxlib::lua_error`.
- `where_error(level, msg)` — concatenates the `push_where` prefix with `msg`
into a `LuaError::Runtime`; mirrors the `luaL_where`+concat pattern in `luaL_error`.
A tiny private `StubBStr` `Display` wrapper was added to keep error formatting allocation-free (private

to this file; `auxlib.rs`'s `BStr` is private to that module).

ly-surfaced todos noticed (NOT in family,

GC family (`gc_count`, `gc_step`, etc.), and metatable/uservalue helpers. These are different

families.

content.text:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **`push_where` location:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:100` (trait default).
- **What it does:** Pushes the source-location prefix string (`short_src:currentline: `) for stack frame `level` onto the stack; empty string if no info. Mirrors `luaL_where` in `lauxlib.c:217`.

**Family members ALSO implemented** (all as `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` overrides in the same file):

- `push_fail` — pushes `LuaValue::Nil` (Lua 5.4 "fail" is nil); mirrors `luaL_pushfail`.
- `push_registry` — pushes `state.registry_value()`; mirrors `lua_pushvalue(L, LUA_REGISTRYINDEX)`.
- `push_upvalue(i)` — `lua_vm::api::push_value(self, upvalue_index(i))`; mirrors `lua_pushvalue(L, lua_upvalueindex(i))`.
- `pop_bytes` — pops and returns the popped value's bytes if it's a `LuaValue::Str`, else `Vec::new()`. Used by `where_error` / `auxlib::lua_error`.
- `where_error(level, msg)` — concatenates the `push_where` prefix with `msg` into a `LuaError::Runtime`; mirrors the `luaL_where`+concat pattern in `luaL_error`.

A tiny private `StubBStr` `Display` wrapper was added to keep error formatting allocation-free (private to this file; `auxlib.rs`'s `BStr` is private to that module).

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed (NOT in family, untouched):** dozens of trait defaults still hit `todo!`, including `get_stack`, `get_info`, `set_top`, `set_hook_full`, `lua_traceback`, the GC family (`gc_count`, `gc_step`, etc.), and metatable/uservalue helpers. These are different families.

**Build:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` ✓ green (warnings only, no errors).

result:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **`push_where` location:** `crates/lua-stdlib/src/state_stub.rs:100` (trait default).
- **What it does:** Pushes the source-location prefix string (`short_src:currentline: `) for stack frame `level` onto the stack; empty string if no info. Mirrors `luaL_where` in `lauxlib.c:217`.

**Family members ALSO implemented** (all as `impl LuaStateStubExt for LuaState` overrides in the same file):

- `push_fail` — pushes `LuaValue::Nil` (Lua 5.4 "fail" is nil); mirrors `luaL_pushfail`.
- `push_registry` — pushes `state.registry_value()`; mirrors `lua_pushvalue(L, LUA_REGISTRYINDEX)`.
- `push_upvalue(i)` — `lua_vm::api::push_value(self, upvalue_index(i))`; mirrors `lua_pushvalue(L, lua_upvalueindex(i))`.
- `pop_bytes` — pops and returns the popped value's bytes if it's a `LuaValue::Str`, else `Vec::new()`. Used by `where_error` / `auxlib::lua_error`.
- `where_error(level, msg)` — concatenates the `push_where` prefix with `msg` into a `LuaError::Runtime`; mirrors the `luaL_where`+concat pattern in `luaL_error`.

A tiny private `StubBStr` `Display` wrapper was added to keep error formatting allocation-free (private to this file; `auxlib.rs`'s `BStr` is private to that module).

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed (NOT in family, untouched):** dozens of trait defaults still hit `todo!`, including `get_stack`, `get_info`, `set_top`, `set_hook_full`, `lua_traceback`, the GC family (`gc_count`, `gc_step`, etc.), and metatable/uservalue helpers. These are different families.

**Build:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` ✓ green (warnings only, no errors).
